settings.text: We know choosing a new dentist is a big decision. At Brightside, we make the process easy — from your first phone call to your first visit, everything is designed to put you at ease.
Your first visit includes a comprehensive exam, digital X-rays, and a personalized treatment plan. We take the time to explain everything clearly so you can make informed decisions about your care.

settings.title: Paperwork & Check-In
settings.description: Complete your forms online before you arrive or fill them in at the office. We'll review your medical history and any concerns you'd like to address.

settings.title: Comprehensive Exam
settings.description: Your dentist performs a thorough examination of your teeth, gums, jaw, and bite. We check for cavities, gum disease, and any signs of oral health issues.

settings.title: Digital X-Rays
settings.description: Low-radiation digital X-rays give us a detailed picture of what's happening below the surface. Results are instant — we review them with you on-screen.

settings.title: Treatment Plan Discussion
settings.description: We sit down together to review findings, discuss options, and create a care plan that fits your goals, timeline, and budget. No surprises, no pressure.

settings.question: What should I bring to my first visit?
settings.answer: Please bring a valid photo ID, your insurance card (if applicable), and a list of any medications you're currently taking. If you completed your forms online, that's all you need.

settings.question: Do you accept my insurance?
settings.answer: We accept most major dental insurance plans including Delta Dental, Cigna, Aetna, MetLife, and Guardian. Not sure about your plan? Call us at (212) 555-0312 and we'll verify your coverage before your visit.

settings.question: How long will my first appointment take?
settings.answer: Plan for about 60 to 90 minutes. This gives us time for a thorough exam, X-rays, cleaning (if needed), and a conversation about your treatment plan without feeling rushed.

settings.question: Do you treat children?
settings.answer: Yes — we're a family practice and welcome patients of all ages. We have experience making dental visits fun and stress-free for kids, and Dr. Chen specializes in pediatric care.

settings.question: What if I have dental anxiety?
settings.answer: You're not alone — many of our patients feel the same way. We offer a gentle, judgment-free environment, clear communication at every step, and sedation options for patients who need extra support.

settings.question: Is parking available?
settings.answer: Yes — there's a parking garage directly next to our building on Park Avenue. We also validate parking for appointments. The office is a 3-minute walk from the 51st Street subway station (6 train).

settings.text: We Can't Wait to Meet You
